The ECB deposit facility rate has been at 2.00% since June 2025 after a cutting cycle from 4%. As of the knowledge cutoff (Nov 9, 2025), the ECB held rates at 2.00% for its third straight meeting (Oct 30, 2025). Market consensus and analyst forecasts strongly suggest the ECB will hold at 2.00% or possibly cut further - not hike. 

For Nov 16: No ECB meeting before this date, so virtually no chance of increase (5%).
For Dec 9: Still before the December ECB meeting (~Dec 11), so virtually no chance of increase (5%).  
For Feb 7, 2026: After potential December and January meetings, but consensus strongly favors holds/cuts, not hikes. Rate hike from 2% would require significant inflation resurgence not forecast in current data (7%).

The FRED trend model's P(>2.00) = 0.71 is misleading here since it compares against prior year's higher values (3.25%), not the policy direction. The actual probability of a rate INCREASE is near the minimum threshold of 0.05 for all dates.

For the February 2026 resolution, uncertainty increases, pulling probability closer to 0.5, but the mild downward trend keeps it at 0.45. Both estimates are near the empirical base rate of 0.422, with the longer horizon slightly elevated due to increased uncertainty.

The FRED statistical model provides a P(exceed) = 0.45 for the Nov 16 horizon, anchoring my estimate. Recent trend in 4-week MA of initial claims is flat after a peak in September (~239k), now at ~227k. The seasonal pattern from prior years shows declining claims in Nov-Dec (in 2024, values fell from ~221k to ~219k in same period), suggesting slight headwind against an increase. The Nov-Dec seasonal decline means Dec 9 is slightly lower probability (~0.40). For Feb 7, 2026, more uncertainty pulls toward 0.5, but Q1 seasonal dip (early 2025 was ~215-218k vs ~227k current) still suggests below-baseline odds, yielding ~0.43. All estimates are near the empirical base rate of 0.422, with uncertainty increasing at longer horizons.

The NDR12MCD series shows a strong, consistent downward trend throughout 2025 (from 1.84 in Nov 2024 to 1.64 in Nov 2025). The comparison baseline is ~1.680 (October 2025 value). The FRED model predicts ~1.62 by December 9, with P(>1.64) = 0.41, but P(>1.68) is approximately 0.08 based on the normal distribution. External evidence confirms CD rates are declining due to Fed policy actions. Adjusted slightly upward to ~0.12 for Dec 9 and ~0.18 for Feb 7 to account for model uncertainty and the instruction that uncertainty increases with horizon. The overall probability of an increase is low given the strong downward trend and gap between current levels and the 1.68 threshold.

The FRED statistical model gives P(value > 1.608) = 0.30 for December 2025. Recent momentum is mostly downward (1 up, 3 down in last 5 periods). For the December 2025 resolution, I anchor at 0.30. For the February 2026 resolution, I increase slightly toward 0.5 due to greater uncertainty over a longer horizon, settling at 0.36. Both are below the empirical base rate of 0.422 due to the persistent downward trend.

The FDIC national savings rate (SNDR) has been on a declining trend from 0.43% in Nov 2024 to 0.40% in Oct-Nov 2025. The FRED statistical model estimates P(value > 0.40) = 0.30 for Dec 9, 2025. The comparison baseline is ~0.400 (value on Nov 9, 2025). Recent slight upward momentum (from 0.38 in June/July back to 0.40) somewhat counterbalances the overall declining trend, but the series has stalled at 0.40. The Fed's rate cut trajectory suggests continued downward pressure on savings rates. For the Dec 9 resolution, I estimate ~0.28 (slightly below the model's 0.30 since an exact tie at 0.400 would not count as an increase). For the Feb 7, 2026 resolution, greater uncertainty pushes toward 0.5 but the downward trend still makes an increase unlikely, so ~0.33.

Probabilities increase appropriately with proximity to baseline and decrease with easing expectations.

The Trump administration rescinded the Biden AI Diffusion Rule on May 13, 2025 (representing a reduction, not expansion, of controls). New guidance was issued simultaneously but guidance doesn't constitute formal export controls in the Federal Register/EAR. The proposed replacement rule ("Framework for Secure Sharing of Advanced AI Technology") was only at the proposed rule stage as of September 2025, with the NPRM deadline already missed. No evidence of new broad AI export control regulations being published in the Federal Register between May-November 2025. The crowd forecast of 0.28 aligns with this analysis; I estimate slightly lower at ~0.22 given the clear policy direction toward rescission/reduction and slow rulemaking progress.

No substantive federal AI regulation meeting the resolution criteria has been enacted as of the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff. The TAKE IT DOWN Act is explicitly excluded per the question background. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act's AI provisions focus primarily on government agency compliance, advisory councils, regulatory sandboxes, and national security supply chain restrictions - not mandatory standards or prohibited uses targeting AI development. Multiple legal experts confirm no comprehensive federal AI regulation exists. With only ~2 months remaining before January 1, 2026, and no comprehensive AI regulation bill near passage, the probability is very low (slightly above the crowd forecast of 0.01 to account for residual uncertainty about OBBBA provisions and any late-session legislation).

The Coalition of the Willing's deployment plans are explicitly contingent on a ceasefire or peace deal being reached first. As of November 2025, no such agreement exists - Russia continues fighting and has maintained maximalist territorial demands. Even in the highly unlikely scenario of a ceasefire being reached in the remaining weeks of 2025, deploying coalition forces would require additional months of legal, political, and logistical preparation. The crowd forecast of 1% aligns with this assessment. There is essentially no realistic path to an actual deployment before December 31, 2025.

Multiple lines of evidence support a YES resolution: (1) UK, Germany, Italy, France have already provided 25 M270 launchers (HIMARS-compatible), representing ongoing formal commitments; (2) Germany made new May 2025 commitments including authorization for 125 long-range rockets and a €5bn deal for joint long-range weapons production with Ukraine; (3) Germany was described in May 2025 as "currently providing Ukraine with M142 HIMARS MLRS and MARS II MLRS"; (4) The Netherlands procured PULS systems beginning delivery in 2025 (though hasn't committed to donating them to Ukraine directly). The resolution criteria requires a formal commitment to M270/HIMARS launchers or substitute systems, which Germany and the UK have already made on multiple occasions. The 0.40 crowd estimate suggests significant uncertainty, possibly because the question may require new 2025 specific commitments rather than continuation of existing ones, or because delivering launchers (not just rockets) may be harder. Given the overwhelming evidence of active European commitment to provide HIMARS-compatible systems and ongoing M270 transfers, I estimate 0.75 probability of YES resolution.

EU quarterly imports of Russian gas are well below 15 bcm in 2025. Q1 2025 was ~10.46 bcm (record low since war), Q2 ~8.84 bcm. Even the peak recent quarter (Q4 2024) was only 13.4 bcm. The Ukraine transit route was eliminated in January 2025, reducing supply by ~3.5 bcm/quarter. There is no realistic path to 15 bcm in any 2025 quarter even with seasonal Q4 demand increases. The trend is firmly downward.

Iran has repeatedly threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz throughout 2025 (June crisis after US nuclear strikes, October sanctions threats) but never followed through. Even after the most severe provocation (US bombing of nuclear sites in June 2025), Iran kept the strait open. The market estimate of 5% on Oct 30 reflects this consensus. Iran has strong self-interest in keeping the strait open (its own oil exports depend on it). Historical precedent shows Iran has never actually closed the strait despite decades of threats. The ceasefire in late June 2025 reduced immediate tensions. While October 2025 sanctions pressure created new threats, no closure had materialized as of the knowledge cutoff (Nov 9, 2025), with ~2 months remaining until the resolution date.

Strong evidence Musk remains Tesla CEO through November 2025: shareholders just approved his massive pay package on Nov 6, he stated in May 2025 he'll stay CEO for 5+ years, Tesla's board firmly denied any departure reports. The market estimate of 0.02 aligns with near-zero probability. Only a tiny residual risk remains for the last ~2 months of 2025.

After 21 of 24 races in 2025, Charles Leclerc has 214 points vs Lewis Hamilton's 148 points - a 66-point gap. With only 3 races (and possibly 1 sprint) remaining, offering a maximum of roughly 80 points, Hamilton would need to dramatically outscore his Ferrari teammate in every remaining event while Leclerc scores near zero. As teammates at the same team, this is extremely unlikely. The market estimate of 0.06 in late October aligned with this view, and the situation has only become slightly worse for Hamilton since then. Probability is approximately 4%.

The US bombed 3 Iranian nuclear sites in June 2025 but this was a limited, targeted strike - not a sustained war. US officials explicitly denied being at war with Iran. A ceasefire was brokered within days, and by August-October 2025, Iran and the US were moving toward nuclear negotiations. The resolution criteria requires sustained military action (regime change campaign or significant ground troop deployment), which did not occur. The market at 4% as of October 30, 2025 reflects the crowd's assessment that this won't resolve YES. With less than 2 months until the resolution date and diplomatic engagement ongoing, the probability of escalation to full-spectrum conflict meeting resolution criteria is very low.

For a YES resolution, both Q3 and Q4 2025 must show negative GDP. Q3 2025 was forecasted at 2-4% annualized growth by all major indicators (Atlanta Fed GDPNow at 3.3-3.9%), making a negative result essentially impossible. Furthermore, the Q4 2025 advance GDP estimate won't be published until late January 2026, after the December 26 resolution date — meaning the question structurally cannot resolve YES regardless of Q4 performance. The Manifold market was at just 2% as of October 30. The probability is very low at ~0.02.

The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee has not declared any recession starting in 2023 or 2024 as of November 2025. The US economy grew positively throughout 2023-2024 with no major contraction. The market prices this at just 1%, reflecting the near-impossibility of an NBER declaration within the remaining weeks of 2025. The most recent NBER recession dates remain February-April 2020. No credible evidence suggests an imminent declaration.

The current official men's marathon world record is 2:00:35 (Kiptum, 2023), still 35+ seconds above the 2-hour barrier. As of the knowledge cutoff (November 9, 2025), all major autumn marathons (London, Boston, World Championships, Chicago, NYC) have concluded without a sub-2 performance. The NYC Marathon on November 2, 2025 was won in 2:08:09. With only ~7 weeks remaining in 2025 and no major world record attempts scheduled, the probability is extremely low. The market estimate of 7% appears too high given the now very limited remaining opportunities.

The S&P 500 dropped 6% on April 4, 2025 (the initial event). After that, evidence shows the market recovered strongly - erasing 2025 losses by mid-May, hitting record highs in July, and trading near ~6,671 in October 2025. No evidence of another 5%+ single-day drop occurred after April 4 through November 9 (the knowledge cutoff). The market estimate of 0.11 on October 30 confirms the market hadn't resolved YES. With ~7 weeks remaining (Nov 9 to Dec 31) and markets near record highs, the probability of such a rare event occurring is approximately 8-10%. A 5%+ single-day drop is historically rare (roughly 14 occurrences in 75 years), typically associated with major crisis events, and the current market environment (stable, near highs) doesn't suggest elevated crash risk for the remainder of 2025.

As of October 2025, the best single-model score on FrontierMath is approximately 29% (GPT-5), with reports indicating AI has hit a "wall" at this level. Even pooling results from multiple frontier models (GPT-5, Gemini 2.5, Grok 4, etc.) only achieves 57% across unlimited attempts. The 80% threshold represents an enormous gap from current performance with only ~2 months remaining in 2025. The Manifold market estimate of 4% is consistent with this assessment. Given the fundamental architectural limits observed and the short remaining timeframe, I estimate this at approximately 3%.

The 49ers are 6-4 after Week 10 (as of Nov 9, 2025), with backup QB Mac Jones starting for at least 5 consecutive games due to Brock Purdy's injury. They trail the 7-2 Rams in the NFC West, just lost badly 42-26 to LA, and are a borderline playoff team. To win Super Bowl LX they'd need to make the playoffs, then win 3+ postseason games. The market was at 7% on Oct 30 when the team was healthier (6-3); post-loss their outlook has deteriorated. A ~6% probability reflects their slim but non-negligible chances of winning the championship.

As of November 9, 2025, Bitcoin is trading at ~$101,600, having declined from its 2025 ATH of ~$125,835 reached in early October. To reach $150,000 by December 31, Bitcoin would need a ~47.6% gain in ~53 days. The Manifold market estimated 15% on October 30 (when BTC was ~$110-115K). Given the further price decline to ~$101.6K, ETF outflows, bearish short-term sentiment, and the fact that $150K would be well beyond the 2025 ATH, I estimate the probability at approximately 10%. While Bitcoin has shown capacity for rapid moves historically, the current conditions (price below previous peak, weak institutional flows) make such a large required gain quite unlikely in the remaining timeframe.

The NFL-ESPN deal was announced (non-binding) in August 2025 and NFL owners approved it October 22, 2025. However, the deal still requires DOJ antitrust clearance (taking up to 12 months) and multiple sources say formal closing won't happen until end of 2026. NFL Media (NFL Network, RedZone, etc.) is still operated by the NFL as of November 2025, not by Disney. The question asks if NFL Media will be "under Disney's control" by end of 2025 - formal legal/operational transfer clearly hasn't happened. The market estimate of 0.87 likely reflects the owner approval as sufficient for their interpretation of "control," but based on strict reading, formal transfer won't occur by January 1, 2026. I estimate ~0.25, acknowledging that some might interpret the signed/approved agreement as "control" but discounting substantially for the clear evidence that the deal won't formally close until 2026.

No evidence of a Waymo SNL sketch through early November 2025 (the knowledge cutoff). The market estimate of 14% as of October 30 reflects relatively low probability. With only a few remaining SNL episodes before January 1, 2026, and no indication one was planned, the probability is approximately 10%. SNL has done tech parodies (ChatGPT, etc.) but hasn't targeted Waymo specifically despite its cultural prominence.

Chrome holds approximately 71-72% of global browser market share as of September-October 2025, far ahead of Safari (~18-19%) and Edge (~4-5%). Chrome's share has been growing throughout 2025, breaking records. There is no credible scenario in which Chrome loses the top position before end of 2025. The market estimate of 97% is well-calibrated.

Elon Musk announced the "America Party" on July 5, 2025, with significant media coverage recognizing it as a political party. However, by late July-August 2025, it was clearly "failing to launch" with no FEC filings, no working website, and no concrete organizational steps. There is an X account with 300k+ followers, but the market creator requires tangible evidence beyond just social media. The market at 0.05 in late October 2025 reflects community consensus that this doesn't meet resolution criteria. Musk appears to have reconciled with Trump and deprioritized the party. There's minimal chance of a major new development before December 31, 2025 given the trajectory.

As of late October 2025, Trump publicly confirmed his relationship with Musk is "good," with the two reconnecting at Charlie Kirk's funeral in September 2025 after their major June 2025 feud. Multiple sources describe Musk as "back in Trump's good graces." Both have strong incentives to maintain positive relations. The main risk is another sudden falling-out, which given the volatile history is possible but unlikely in the remaining ~2 months. The market estimate of 0.83 aligns with the evidence.

Despite South Park aggressively mocking Trump throughout Season 27 (July-late October 2025), no legal action has been initiated. The White House response was limited to verbal criticism. Legal experts unanimously say First Amendment protections make any legal action unlikely to succeed. South Park has actually thrived, signing a $1.5B deal. With only ~2 months left in the resolution period and no action taken through the forecast date of Nov 9, 2025, the probability of formal legal action with negative consequences by Dec 31 is very low. The market estimate of 0.23 seems too high; I estimate ~0.07.

Despite the significant Israel-Iran war in June 2025 and internal power struggles, the Iranian regime has maintained control. Khamenei reappeared publicly on October 20, 2025, after a month's absence. The regime is actively strengthening institutional resilience (Defense Council creation, key appointments). With only ~2 months remaining until the resolution date and no credible imminent threat to regime survival, coupled with the market consensus of 3%, the probability of YES is very low.

The probability of a technical recession (two consecutive quarters of negative GDP growth) is very low. Q1 2025 was the only quarter with negative growth (-0.5%), while Q2 2025 was strongly positive (3.0-3.8%). Q3 2025 is forecast by multiple sources to be solidly positive (2.3-4.0% range). For a YES resolution, we'd need Q3 AND Q4 2025 to both be negative, which is inconsistent with all available data. The market estimate of 4% on October 30, 2025 aligns with this assessment. Even if Q4 2025 faces headwinds from a government shutdown, Q3 2025 appears solidly positive, making two consecutive negative quarters extremely unlikely.

FiveThirtyEight/538 was definitively shut down in March 2025 as part of Disney/ABC News cost-cutting layoffs. There is no evidence of any plans to acquire, revive, or sell the brand. The NYT picked up some polling data work but not the brand itself. With only ~2 months left before the January 1, 2026 resolution date and no signals of revival, the probability is very low, consistent with the market estimate of ~2%.

Manifold already launched a sweepstakes ("sweepcash") model in September 2024 allowing real USD prizes in most US states, but this is not traditional real-money gambling. The question appears to be tracking whether Manifold will offer actual real-money betting (possibly crypto-based) through a dedicated gambling platform. The market price of 0.18 on October 30, 2025 - from a community of Manifold insiders who know the platform well - strongly suggests this hasn't happened and is unlikely to happen before year-end 2025. CEO Stevo's mention of crypto plans appears to not have materialized into an actual launch by the forecast date. I estimate ~0.16 probability of YES resolution.

BLS/FRED data shows federal employment was only down ~97,000 from January 2025 as of August 2025. Monthly decline rate was slowing (~14,000/month recent). To reach 250,000 fewer employees by December 17, 2025 (when November 2025 data would be visible on FRED), the pace would need to nearly triple. The government shutdown in late September/October could have caused a statistical anomaly in October data (if workers were on unpaid furlough during the October 12 reference week), but: (1) workers on paid administrative leave still count as employed in BLS survey, (2) November data would recover from any shutdown effects, and (3) some agencies were rehiring. OPM's 300,000 projection exceeds BLS counts because BLS counts paid leave as employment. The market estimate of 0.46 is surprisingly high, but the hard BLS data trajectory strongly suggests the 250,000 threshold will not be reached on the FRED chart by the resolution date.

Elon Musk announced the "America Party" in early July 2025 but never filed official FEC paperwork himself. By July 30, 2025, reports characterized the effort as "failing to launch." The Trump-Musk feud that motivated the announcement appeared to be easing. The resolution criteria requires both public announcement AND official FEC filing - while Musk made a public announcement, no official FEC filing was made. The market estimate of 5% as of October 30, 2025 reflects near-zero momentum. With the deadline being December 31, 2025 and no visible progress in months, the probability is very low.

Multiple perpetrators directly involved in the October 19 Louvre robbery were arrested on October 25, 2025 - well before Christmas. Two suspects partially admitted their role, were formally charged with organized theft, and were found in possession of stolen items. Additional suspects were charged by November 1. As of November 9, there is no evidence of exoneration. The probability of YES resolution is very high (~0.95), consistent with the Manifold market price of 0.95 on October 30.

Multiple strong barriers prevent this from resolving YES: (1) WGA rules prohibit studios from having AI write scripts for covered productions; (2) Hollywood is actively hiding AI use rather than acknowledging it; (3) Paul Schrader (Oct 2025) says first fully AI film is "two years away"; (4) AI-generated films currently have tiny budgets ($500K range), far below the $100M threshold; (5) Only ~7 weeks remain in 2025 for a qualifying film to open in theaters. The market estimate of 7% is already low, but given these compounding barriers, I estimate ~3% probability.

As of November 2025, there is no evidence that AGI as smart as a "generally educated human" has been achieved. Expert consensus and aggregate forecasts push AGI milestones to 2027-2028 at earliest (50% probability). Current AI systems, while impressive, are not widely considered to meet the bar for general human-level intelligence. The market estimate of 7% is well-calibrated. The resolution mechanism (a public poll) further requires broad consensus, making YES resolution even less likely. With only ~7 weeks between the knowledge cutoff and resolution date, the probability remains very low at approximately 6%.

Virgin Galactic has $508 million in cash as of June 30, 2025, with a declining burn rate (~$60M/quarter in Q2 2025). The resolution date is January 1, 2026, which is only ~6 months after the latest available financial data. With hundreds of millions in cash and a declining burn rate, there is virtually no risk of bankruptcy before January 1, 2026. No bankruptcy filing was reported through the November 9, 2025 knowledge cutoff.

As of November 2025, China has no confirmed military bases in any BRICS member country. China's overseas bases include Djibouti (not BRICS) and the Ream joint logistics center in Cambodia (not a BRICS member). BRICS members include Brazil, Russia, India, South Africa, Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE, Indonesia, and Saudi Arabia. While China has conducted joint exercises in Egypt and explored basing options in UAE, no military base has been established or officially announced in any BRICS country. With less than 2 months remaining to the January 1, 2026 resolution date, and no signals of an imminent announcement, the probability is very low. The community estimate of 8% seems slightly high; I estimate approximately 5%.

As of August 2025, the Swiss Federal Tribunal had not yet ruled on Jordan Chiles' appeal. Historical success rates for appeals against CAS decisions are extremely low (~1%). Even a favorable ruling would likely remand to CAS rather than directly overturn. There are approximately 4-5 months remaining before the January 1, 2026 resolution date. While the novel procedural arguments (conflict of interest, incorrect email notifications) provide slightly better odds than average, the overall probability of resolution in Chiles' favor before 2026 remains very low.

As of the most recent CDC IRAT assessments (May 2025), H5N1 viruses score 5.21-5.59 for potential emergence risk, well below the 8.0 threshold for "high" risk. Historical assessments have consistently placed H5 viruses in the moderate range (4.0-7.9). The highest emergence score ever recorded by IRAT is 7.5 (for an H1N1 swine strain). For an H5 virus to reach 8.0 before January 1, 2026, it would need a dramatic jump of over 2 points - requiring evidence of efficient human-to-human transmission, which has not been observed. With only ~2 months remaining and no signs of imminent change, the probability is extremely low.

As of early November 2025, Ne Zha 2 (a sequel/franchise film) leads 2025 worldwide box office with ~$1.9 billion. All other top films are also franchises. With the resolution date of November 29, 2025, Avatar: Fire and Ash (Christmas release) won't factor in. There is essentially zero chance a non-franchise film could overtake Ne Zha 2's $1.9B lead in 3 weeks. Every film currently in the top 10 of 2025 is part of a franchise, series, or established IP. Probability is extremely high, limited only by unforeseen extraordinary circumstances.

The evidence is conclusive. GTA VI was delayed out of 2025 entirely:
1. In May 2025, Rockstar/Take-Two announced the first delay from Fall 2025 to May 26, 2026.
2. In November 2025 (before the knowledge cutoff), a second delay was announced pushing the game to November 19, 2026.

The game will not be available for sale in Europe (or anywhere) in 2025. The resolution date is November 30, 2025, and GTA VI will not launch before then. The community prediction of 0.00 on October 30, 2025 reflects this certainty. I assign the minimum allowed probability of 0.02 to account for extreme unforeseen events.

Spain's GDP grew 2.8% year-on-year in Q3 2025 (INE official data, Oct 29, 2025), while the Eurozone grew 1.3% year-on-year in Q3 2025 (Eurostat flash estimate, Oct 30, 2025). Spain clearly outperformed the Eurozone by 1.5 percentage points. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), signed July 4, 2025, was the main reconciliation legislation modifying IRA energy tax credits. The bill's approach toward wind/solar under 45Y was instead to accelerate phase-out (requiring placement in service by end of 2027) and add FEOC restrictions. No enacted legislation makes domestic content mandatory for base 45Y eligibility. With Congress having completed its major reconciliation push and limited legislative time remaining in 2025, the probability of this specific change occurring before January 1, 2026 is extremely low.

However, the key uncertainty is timing: the community prediction was 50% on October 30, suggesting cert hadn't been granted yet. The SG typically takes 3-6 months to respond to CVSGs, meaning a response around September-December 2025, with the Court then needing additional time to conference and decide before January 15, 2026. The tight timeline between the SG's likely response and the resolution date creates meaningful uncertainty. The U.S. Chamber filing both cert and merits amicus briefs is a possible signal that cert was granted, but this couldn't be confirmed. Balancing the strong CVSG signal with timing uncertainty, I estimate approximately 58% probability.
